name: @radzor/circuit-breaker
description: Circuit breaker pattern for fault-tolerant external service calls. Tracks failure rates, opens the circuit to fast-fail requests when a threshold is exceeded, and probes recovery with half-open state. Zero dependencies.

inputs.name: threshold
inputs.description: Number of consecutive failures before the circuit opens.

inputs.name: timeout
inputs.description: Time in milliseconds the circuit stays open before moving to half-open.

inputs.name: halfOpenRequests
inputs.description: Number of probe requests allowed in half-open state.

inputs.name: volumeThreshold
inputs.description: Minimum number of requests in a window before the failure rate is evaluated.

outputs.name: metrics
outputs.type: { totalRequests: number; failures: number; successes: number; lastFailureAt: number | null }

events.name: onOpen
events.description: Fired when the circuit opens due to too many failures.

events.name: onHalfOpen
events.description: Fired when the circuit transitions to half-open to probe recovery.

events.name: onClose
events.description: Fired when the circuit closes (service recovered).

events.name: onRejected
events.description: Fired when a request is fast-failed because the circuit is open.

actions.name: execute

params.name: fn
params.description: Async function to protect with the circuit breaker.

actions.description: Execute a function through the circuit breaker. Throws CircuitOpenError if the circuit is open.

actions.name: getMetrics
actions.returns: { totalRequests: number; failures: number; successes: number; lastFailureAt: number | null }

actions.name: reset
actions.description: Manually reset the circuit to closed state and clear metrics.

llm.constraints: Create one circuit breaker instance per external dependency, not per request. State is in-memory — resets on process restart. Wrap @radzor/http-client calls or any external service call inside execute().
